titleStmt.title: SII 17.719: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 719. (A.R. No. 662 of 1904.) TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa figuring in this inscription is the well-known feudatory of Rājarāja III and the year 35 cited may perhaps be taken as an year of Rājarājā's reign, i.e., 1251-52 A.D.

This inscription registers the order of Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻaṉ granting the village Tirumullaivāyal free from the payment of taxes such as poṉvari, nāṭṭuvari, kāṇikkai, uḷḷūr-puṟa-kkalaṉai, etc., for conducting a festival and a service called after Vijaya Gaṉḍagōpālaṉ and for pūja and other food-offerings to the deity Tirumullaivāyal-uḍaiya Nāyaṉār n Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu, in Puḻal-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . maturāntakap pottappic coḻaṉ Olai 3 10 5 ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu puḻal koṭṭattu
kāṉappeṟur nāṭṭu Uṭaiyār tirumullaivāyaluṭaiya nāyaṉārkku vicaiyakaṇṭakopālaṉ cittirait ti
runāḷ Eḻuntaruḷavum vicaiyakaṇṭakopālaṉ canti Amutuceyyavum pūcaikkum Amutupaṭikkum tiru
mullaivāyal poṉvari kaṭamai nāṭṭuvari kāṇikkai Uḷḷūrppuṟakkalaṉai maṟṟum Epperp paṭṭa varikaḷum
Aṉaittu Āyam Iḻakka sarvvamāṉiyamāka śantrādittavaraiyum cel(la)vatāka viṭṭom Ippaṭi ceyva
te Ivai kaṇṭakopālaṉ Eḻuttu .
Digital edition of SII 17.719 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
